description: Given an array of strings, group the anagrams together. You may return the answer in any order; the order of the groups and the order of words within a group do not matter. Input: first line n, then n whitespace-separated words. Output: each group on its own line, words separated by a space.
Given an array of integers, return all values that appear more than once, each value once, in any order. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the duplicate values separated by spaces.

Given distinct positive integers, return the largest subset such that every pair (a,b) satisfies a%b==0 or b%a==0. If several subsets of the maximum size exist, return any one of them. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the subset elements separated by spaces.

Given an array and a target, return the indices (i, j) of two elements that sum to the target. If multiple such pairs exist, return any one. Input: first line n and target, then n integers. Output: the two indices space-separated.

Given coin denominations and an amount, return ANY combination of coins (with repetition) of MINIMUM count that sums to the amount. If impossible, print -1. Input: first line k and amount, then k denominations. Output: the chosen coins space-separated.
Given an array of n integers, return their sum. There is exactly one correct answer. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the sum.
